titleStmt.title: SII 23.588: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 588. (A.R. No. 588 of 1907.) Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District Saumyanatha temple—on the south wall of the central shrine Sundara Pāṇḍya. Year 17: 1292-93 A.D.

summary.p: This seems to be a record of Jaṭāvarman Sundara-Pāṇḍya II (acc. A.D. 1276). The details of the date are given as Nandana, Mīna, śu. 10, Wednesday, Pushya which would correspond to A.D. 1293; The record states that on this day one Karumāṇikkāḻvāṉ [Ja]ndivarmmar the headman of Śiṟumali residing at Toḻūr in Toḻūrnāḍu a division of Maṇavil-kōṭṭam in Jayangoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam caused to be given the sacred bath (tirumañjanam) to Śokkapperumāḷ and his consorts in the temple at Nirandanūr. The special mention of the bath probably signifies a purificatory reconsecration of the deities.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīsundarapāṇḍyadevarkku
yāṇṭu 10 7Āvatu nandanasamvatsarattu miṉṉāyaṟṟu pūrva
pakṣattu daśamiyum budhankiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattu ṉā
ḷatirājendramaṇḍalattu meṟpākkaiṉāṭṭu niran=ta
ṉūrāna Akarabrahmadeyam śrīkulottuṅka coḻa
ccaturvedimaṅkalattu kulottuṅkacoḻa viṇṇaka
r Emperumāṉ cokkap perumāḷaiyum nācci
māraiyum jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇḍalattu maṇa
vilkoṭṭattut toḻurnāṭṭut toḻur ciṟu
malikiḻāṉ karumāṇikkāḻvāṉ [ja]ndivarmmar tiru
mañcanam paṇṇiṉār .—
Digital edition of SII 23.588 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
